message.content:
<|channel>thought<channel|>### C# API Design: Resumable Model Benchmark Runner

To handle the "one heavy model server at a time" constraint while allowing resumability, the design uses a **Sequential Task Queue** pattern with a persistent state store.

#### 1. Core API Sketches

```csharp
// Represents the current progress of a specific benchmark run
public record RunState(
    string RunId, 
    BenchmarkContext Context, 
    int LastCompletedStepIndex, 
    double? FinalScore);

public interface IBenchmarkRunner
{
    // Starts or resumes a suite of benchmarks
    Task RunSuiteAsync(IEnumerable<BenchmarkContext> contexts, CancellationToken ct);
    
    // Returns the current status of the runner
    RunState GetCurrentState();
}

public interface IBenchmarkEngine
{
    // The actual execution logic (Docker harness interaction)
    // Called sequentially to respect the "one heavy model" constraint
    Task<double[]> ExecuteStepAsync(BenchmarkContext context, int stepIndex, CancellationToken ct);
}

public interface IStateRepository
{
    Task SaveStateAsync(RunState state);
    Task<RunState?> LoadLastStateAsync();
}

// Implementation Sketch
public class ResumableRunner : IBenchmarkRunner
{
    private readonly IBenchmarkEngine _engine;
    private readonly IStateRepository _repository;
    private RunState _currentState;

    public ResumableRunner(IBenchmarkEngine engine, IStateRepository repository)
    {
        _engine = engine;
        _repository = repository;
    }

    public async Task RunSuiteAsync(IEnumerable<BenchmarkContext> contexts, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        // 1. Attempt Resumption
        var saved = await _repository.Load
